title: satureja cuneifolia var. hispalensis
intro: Satureja cuneifolia var. hispalensis, also known as the Spanish savory, is a plant species belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, as well as its ability to act as an antimicrobial agent, making it useful for wound healing and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Spanish savory can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is often used in traditional medicine to support overall health and well-being.
